# Phase 2.2 DOC System Frontend Specialist Agent

This file is the human-facing entry brief for the workspace's independent DOC-system frontend specialist builder agent.

## Purpose

Use this agent when you want an independent frontend-specialist build pass that creates the initial site theme, shared surfaces, reusable sections, blocks, and core components directly inside the chosen `FRONTEND DEV` project root.

It is intended to solve the weak initial frontend-building problem where generic sections, simple layouts, and uninspired styling keep appearing. It is not a screenshot replicator and not a sibling-project or reference-style scanner.

## What It Owns

- independent startup theme building inside one target `FRONTEND DEV` root
- overall site design theme and shared visual system
- reusable sections, page blocks, and continuation-ready component foundation
- stronger frontend-specialist execution quality for the first real site build
- maintainable Next.js frontend structure and validation

## What It Does Not Own

- screenshot replication
- sibling project comparison unless explicitly approved by the user
- reference-style folder inspection unless explicitly approved by the user
- backend or deploy lane work
- repo-topology or system-governance work

## Actual Invocable Agent

- Public wrapper: `.github/agents/phase2.2-doc-system-frontend-specialist.agent.md`

## Supporting Files

- Specialist spec: `.github/doc-system-phase2-2-frontend-specialist/phase2.2-doc-system-frontend-specialist-spec.md`
- Readiness checklist: `.github/doc-system-phase2-2-frontend-specialist/phase2.2-doc-system-frontend-specialist-readiness-checklist.md`
- Shared starter source: `.github/project-starters/hybrid-canonical-project-starter/`

## Rule Of Thumb

Use `phase2-doc-system-frontend-dev.agent.md` for the standard DOC-system transformation pass.

Use `phase2.1-doc-system-frontend-dev.agent.md` when you want a transformation pass with stronger frontend-director execution quality.

Use this Phase 2.2 agent when you want an independent frontend specialist to build the initial startup theme, sections, blocks, and reusable site foundation directly inside the target `FRONTEND DEV` root without wandering through unrelated references.
